content.claim: Students in Hungarian gimnázium general secondary schools perform at the international level of top PISA performers such as Switzerland, Japan, and Korea, demonstrating that early-track selection serves high-performing students at an elite level.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: This finding addresses distributional effects for selected students but does not address equity impacts on non-selected students or system-wide inequality measures
Performance at the elite level is consistent with both selective and non-selective systems
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
